title: royena hirsuta var. rugosa
intro: Royena hirsuta var. rugosa, also known as the hairy dogwood or hairy cornelian cherry, is a plant species in the family Cornaceae. It is a deciduous shrub with medicinal properties due to its bark, leaves, and roots, which are rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, offering benefits such as reduced pain, improved digestion, and boosted immune system. The herbal preparations of Royena hirsuta var. rugosa include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, syrups, and topical creams.
